intro: Expedition encounters involve 4 ancient factions. Each faction has a Logbook faction, a merchant NPC, a unique trade currency, and specialised rewards. Knowing which faction to prioritise dramatically increases your return on Expedition investment.

factions.name: Kalguuran Expedition
merchant_offers: Rog: reforges items using currency orbs — costs Exotic Coinage + the orbs
Can reforge rare items with Orbs of Alteration, Chaos, Exalted, and more
High-ilvl item reforge is one of the strongest crafting options in the game
Upgrades quality and links via Rog for cost savings vs orb spam
farming_tips: Stack Kalguuran remnant keywords on Atlas passives for more Exotic Coinage
Target detonation paths through Kalguuran soldier graves (yellow outlines)
Rog reforging scales with item level — always use ilvl 82+ bases
Save Exotic Coinage for Rog crafting rather than trading bulk — better value
factions.notes: Most popular Expedition faction for endgame currency. Rog crafting can produce mirror-tier items.

factions.name: Druids of the Broken Circle
merchant_offers: Dannig: exchanges Astragali for Atlas passive respec orbs and league-specific currencies
Grand Architect's Seal: used to upgrade Atlas regions
Orb of Conflict: upgrades one influence mod while downgrading another
Bulk Orbs of Unmaking for Atlas respec without spending Regret Orbs
farming_tips: Orbs of Unmaking are most consistent currency gain per logbook
Target druid ritual circle relics (blue glowing circles) for Astragali bonuses
Druid logbooks often appear in Acts 1–2 area levels — easy to complete
Use Orbs of Conflict on influenced items when one mod tier is already maxed
factions.notes: Best faction for Atlas respec flexibility. Orbs of Unmaking have reliable bulk trade value.

factions.name: Black Scythe Mercenaries
merchant_offers: Tujen: blind haggling — offer artifacts for mystery currency, haggle down for profit
Top-tier offers include Divine Orbs, Exalted Orbs, Annulments
Haggling down 2–3 times typically still yields profit — don't over-haggle
Tujen can offer mirror-tier currency at ultra-rare rates
farming_tips: Black Scythe Artifacts are the primary Tujen currency — stack them before trading
Haggle offers with 'I'll take this for less' — accept when 20–30% below ask
Clicking 'No' ends all offers — only decline if the offer is clearly bad
Stack at least 100+ Artifacts before visiting Tujen for best session value
factions.notes: Highest variance faction — can yield Mirror-tier currency. Tujen haggling requires practice to maximise. Most exciting faction to farm.

factions.name: Order of the Chalice
merchant_offers: Gwennen: gamble Sun Artifacts for unique items — slot is shown, specific unique is random
Can produce chase uniques (Headhunter, Mageblood, Voices) from their respective slots
Helmet slot: chance at Mageblood and other meta helmets
Weapon slot: chance at Paradoxica, Cospri's Malice, etc.
farming_tips: Save Sun Artifacts until you have 200+ before gambling — larger samples reduce variance
Focus gambling on high-value slots: Belt (Headhunter), Helmet (Mageblood), Amulet (Voices)
Order logbooks often appear in Act 4–6 areas — harder but more rewards per detonation
Gwennen has no minimum ilvl requirement — always gamble on the desired slot
factions.notes: Highest risk, highest reward faction for item hunting. Mageblood and Headhunter can drop from Gwennen. Most popular for league start unique hunting.

expedition_mechanics: Place explosives along detonation chain — each charge affects adjacent relics
Runic Monsters (with remnant keywords active) drop faction artifacts on death
Remnant keywords modify encounter rules — some add bonuses, some add dangers
Logbook difficulty scales with area level — use high-ilvl maps for better rewards
Artifact quantity scales with number of relics detonated per run — maximise chain length
tips: Each faction's logbook drops in specific atlas regions — check the map device filter
Remnant keywords can stack — multiple 'Runic Monsters drop extra artifacts' keywords multiply yield
All 4 merchants reset inventory each time you enter the Expedition encounter area
You can run multiple logbooks in sequence — merchants persist between encounters in the same session
